message:
fix(combinator): reject impossible requests early in bulkhead and rate limiter

bulkhead.rs: Reject enqueue requests whose weight exceeds
max_concurrent immediately with BulkheadError::Full. Previously,
such requests would enter the queue but could never be admitted
(no amount of draining could free enough capacity), leading to
queue slot waste and eventual timeout. The early rejection
increments total_rejected for accurate metrics.

rate_limit.rs: Reject acquire/wait requests whose cost exceeds
the burst capacity immediately. A request that needs more tokens
than the bucket can ever hold will never succeed regardless of
refill—waiting would block indefinitely or until timeout. Changes:
  - time_until_available: returns Duration::MAX for cost > burst
    (moved cost_f computation before the lock to avoid holding it
    unnecessarily on the fast-reject path)
  - acquire_or_enqueue: returns RateLimitExceeded for cost > burst
    before checking the wait strategy, incrementing total_rejected

Both changes prevent resource exhaustion from pathological inputs
and make the rejection path O(1) with no lock acquisition needed.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
